quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: Hungarian paprika, the beloved spice that gives goulash its distinctive flavor and color, comes in eight different grades ranging from delicate and sweet to extremely hot. Hungary produces some of the world's finest paprika, particularly from the regions of Szeged and Kalocsa, where the peppers are still dried and ground using traditional methods.

short_read.title: The Return of Sea Otters to the Strait of Juan de Fuca
short_read.teaser: After nearly disappearing from Washington waters, sea otters are slowly making a comeback along the Olympic Peninsula. These playful marine mammals are not just adorable—they're essential to keeping kelp forests healthy.

medium_read.title: How Plants Communicate Through Underground Networks
medium_read.teaser: Scientists have discovered that plants use vast underground fungal networks—nicknamed the 'Wood Wide Web'—to share nutrients and even warning signals about threats. Your garden may be more connected than you ever imagined.

long_read.title: Budapest: The City of Healing Waters and Grand History
long_read.teaser: From Roman times to the present, thermal baths have been central to Hungarian culture and life in Budapest. This beautifully illustrated story explores the city's famous spas, architecture, and the role these healing waters have played through centuries of history.

port_angeles.title: Olympic National Park's Roosevelt Elk
port_angeles.description: Just minutes from Port Angeles, Olympic National Park is home to one of the largest herds of Roosevelt elk in the world. These magnificent animals, named after President Theodore Roosevelt, can often be spotted grazing in meadows, especially in the Hoh Rain Forest and near Hurricane Ridge. Summer is a wonderful time to see them with their calves.

puzzle_1.question: A hummingbird's heart can beat up to ___ times per minute during flight.
puzzle_1.correct_response: Exactly right! That tiny heart works incredibly hard to keep those wings humming!
puzzle_1.close_response: You're very close! Their amazing little hearts beat about 1,200 times per minute—that's 20 times per second!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: Those tiny hearts are even faster than that—about 1,200 beats per minute! It's hard to imagine something so small working so hard.
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: Hummingbirds have the highest metabolism of all animals. To fuel their rapid flight and heartbeat, they must eat about half their body weight in sugar daily, visiting hundreds of flowers.

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'thank you'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Köszönöm! That's exactly right—and you probably said it many times growing up!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The word for 'thank you' is 'köszönöm'—such a warm and familiar word from your Hungarian roots!
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: Hungarian, or Magyar, is unique among European languages—it's part of the Finno-Ugric family and is more closely related to Finnish than to any neighboring language. Its distinctive sounds and structure make it one of the world's most fascinating languages.

puzzle_3.question: What do harbor seals use their whiskers for in the dark waters of the Strait of Juan de Fuca?
options: Finding their way back to shore
puzzle_3.correct_response: Perfect! Those sensitive whiskers can detect the tiniest vibrations from fish swimming nearby—like underwater motion detectors. Harbor seals you might see near Port Angeles use this superpower to hunt even in murky water!
puzzle_3.wrong_response: Actually, they use those impressive whiskers to detect fish movements through vibrations in the water! The harbor seals around Port Angeles rely on this amazing sense to hunt successfully, even when they can't see well.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: Harbor seals are common residents of the Strait of Juan de Fuca and Puget Sound. Their whiskers are so sensitive they can follow the trail a fish left behind minutes earlier, making them excellent hunters in the Pacific Northwest's often cloudy waters.

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
